rationale: The citing paper uses Galak, Small, and Stephen (2011) to motivate its identifiable-victim argument and explicitly cites their finding that lenders are less likely to lend as recipient group size increases. It then develops and tests a parallel prediction (stronger effects for single-person creators) in its own hypotheses and interpretation. The paper also explicitly contrasts contexts, arguing that its reward-based crowdfunding setting with financial incentives extends prior prosocial lending evidence.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Galak et al. (2011) as prior field evidence on identifiable-victim effects in prosocial lending and tests a related mechanism in Kickstarter data. It positions its contribution as extending that line of work to reward-based crowdfunding, where backers also expect products/services in return.
